meals.title: Three Homemade Dressings for Meal Prep
meals.description: Having a few good dressings ready in the fridge is a total game changer. When flavor is already handled, throwing together salads and bowls during the week becomes fast and effortless instead of feeling like an afterthought. This is one of those habits that makes eating at home feel easier and way more exciting. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 2 whole-food ingredient swap(s).
steps: Step 1: Add all ingredients to a large jar.
Step 2: Blend with an immersion blender or shake and whisk until fully emulsified.
Step 3: Taste and adjust salt, lemon, or sumac as needed.
Step 4: Transfer to an airtight container and store in the fridge for up to 5 to 7 days.
Step 5: Add all ingredients to a large jar.
Step 6: Blend with an immersion blender or transfer to a regular blender and blend until smooth and fully emulsified.
Step 7: Taste and adjust sweetness or heat as needed.
Step 8: Transfer to an airtight container and store in the fridge for up to 5 to 7 days.
Step 9: Add all ingredients to a large jar.
Step 10: Blend with an immersion blender or transfer to a regular blender and blend until smooth, thick, and fully emulsified.
Step 11: Taste and adjust salt or vinegar as needed.
Step 12: Transfer to an airtight container and store in the fridge for up to 5 to 7 days.

meals.title: Four Essential Sauces for Meal Prep
meals.description: Food is always better with sauce so let’s meal prep four sauces that you can keep in the fridge at all times! You can make one, two, three, or all four at once depending on how much you love sauce. There’s a sauce here for everyone to love so no more dry sandwiches or rice bowls! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 1 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons cotija or parmesan cheese optional

steps: Step 1: Add all the ingredients for the cilantro jalapeño sauce into a food processor or blender.
Step 2: Blend until smooth and creamy and transfer to an air-tight jar to store in the fridge!.
Step 3: Combine all the ingredients in a bowl until smooth and creamy.
Step 4: Transfer to an air-tight jar to store in the fridge!.
Step 5: Combine all the ingredients in a bowl with a spoon or fork until smooth and creamy.
Step 6: Transfer to an air-tight jar to store in the fridge!.
Step 7: Add all the ingredients for the white sauce into a food processor or blender.
Step 8: Blend until smooth and creamy and transfer to an air-tight jar to store in the fridge!.

meals.title: Five Bold Chicken Marinades
meals.description: Life gets busy, and sometimes the last thing you want to do after a long day is stand in the kitchen figuring out dinner. That’s exactly why I love prepping chicken marinades ahead of time and freezing them for when you need a quick, flavorful meal on the fly. These five chicken marinades bring totally different vibes — from creamy lemon herb to spicy shawarma and fragrant Thai green curry. Each one packs serious flavor, and with a little advance prep, you’ll have ready-to-cook chicken waiting in your freezer for any night of the week. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 4 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: chicken thighs or breast (whole or chopped)
chicken thighs or breast (whole or chopped)
chicken thighs or breast (whole or chopped)
chicken thighs or breast (whole or chopped)
chicken thighs or breast (whole or chopped)

steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, cilantro, parsley, mustard, garlic spread, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Step 2: Add chicken and coat them well in the marinade.
Step 3: Transfer the chicken and marinade to a freezer-safe bag or container, press out excess air, and seal.
Step 4: Label and freeze for up to 3 months.
Step 5: To thaw: Place in the fridge overnight, or submerge sealed bag in cold water for a quicker thaw (about 1–2 hours).
Step 6: When ready to cook, grill, air fry, bake, or sear until the chicken is cooked through and golden.
Step 7: In a mixing bowl, combine olive oil, harissa, tomato paste, garlic, allspice, coriander, paprika, cumin, salt, sumac, black pepper, and turmeric.
Step 8: Whisk into a thick, spicy marinade.
Step 9: Add chicken and toss to coat evenly.
Step 10: Transfer the chicken and marinade to a freezer-safe bag or container, press out excess air, and seal.
Step 11: Label and freeze for up to 3 months.
Step 12: To thaw: Place in the fridge overnight, or submerge sealed bag in cold water for a quicker thaw (about 1–2 hours).
Step 13: Cook by searing in a hot pan, air frying, or grilling until tender and charred at the edges.
Step 14: In a bowl, mix together miso paste, rice vinegar, sesame oil, honey, garlic, ginger paste, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper until smooth.
Step 15: Add chicken and turn to coat them in the marinade.
Step 16: Transfer the chicken and marinade to a freezer-safe bag or container, press out excess air, and seal.
Step 17: Label and freeze for up to 3 months.
Step 18: To thaw: Place in the fridge overnight, or submerge sealed bag in cold water for a quicker thaw (about 1–2 hours).
Step 19: Cook by baking, air frying, or pan-searing until caramelized and fully cooked.
Step 20: In a large bowl, combine oil, orange juice, lime juice, soy sauce, paprika, cumin, oregano, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne, and black pepper.

meals.title: Five Freezer-Ready Chicken Marinades
meals.description: If there’s one thing that instantly makes weeknight cooking feel less overwhelming, it’s having protein already prepped and waiting for you. These freezer chicken marinades are something I always come back to when life gets busy but I still want flavorful, home-cooked meals. Everything gets mixed in a bowl or directly in a zip-top bag, massaged and shaken around, labeled, and popped into the freezer. Future you will be very grateful. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 4 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: chicken thighs or breast, chopped or whole
chicken thighs or breast, chopped or whole
chicken thighs or breast, chopped or whole
chicken thighs or breast, chopped or whole
chicken thighs or breast, chopped or whole

steps: Step 1: To make the saffron water, place the saffron threads in a small bowl, top with a few ice cubes, and let the ice melt completely.

Step 2: Add all marinade ingredients (including bloomed saffron) to a bowl or directly into a zip-top bag and mix together. Add the chicken and toss until evenly coated. If you used a bowl, transfer to the ziploc bag now.
Step 3: Seal the bag tightly, pressing out as much air as possible, then massage and shake around to coat the chicken evenly.
Step 4: Label the bag with the marinade name and date, then place flat in the freezer.
Step 5: Add all marinade ingredients to a bowl or directly into a zip-top bag and mix together. Add the chicken and toss until evenly coated. If you used a bowl, transfer to the ziploc bag now.
Step 6: Seal the bag tightly, pressing out excess air, then massage and shake around until the chicken is fully coated.
Step 7: Label with the marinade name and date, then place flat in the freezer.
Step 8: Add all marinade ingredients to a bowl or directly into a zip-top bag and mix together. Add the chicken and toss until evenly coated. If you used a bowl, transfer to the ziploc bag now.
Step 9: Seal tightly, removing as much air as possible, then massage and shake around to evenly coat the chicken.
Step 10: Label with the marinade name and date, then place flat in the freezer.
Step 11: Add all marinade ingredients to a bowl or directly into a zip-top bag and mix together. Add the chicken and toss until evenly coated. If you used a bowl, transfer to the ziploc bag now.
Step 12: Seal tightly, pressing out excess air, then massage and shake around until evenly coated.
Step 13: Label with the marinade name and date, then place flat in the freezer.
Step 14: Add all marinade ingredients to a bowl or directly into a zip-top bag and mix together. Add the chicken and toss until evenly coated. If you used a bowl, transfer to the ziploc bag now.
Step 15: Seal tightly, removing excess air, then massage and shake around to coat evenly.
Step 16: Label with the marinade name and date, then place flat in the freezer.

meals.title: Air Fryer Gochujang Chicken Skewers
meals.description: If you’re bored of plain chicken and rice, these Korean-inspired skewers are about to change the game. They’re sweet, spicy, and so addicting with that gochujang marinade. Made in the air fryer, they cook up fast with the perfect charred, smoky flavor that takes them over the top. Whether you’re grilling them, baking them in the oven, or air frying, they’re an easy, bold dinner that’s ready in 30 minutes. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 2 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: chicken thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces

steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, whisk together the gochujang, brown sugar, sesame oil, rice vinegar, garlic, ginger paste, smoked paprika, gochugaru, soy sauce, and salt until smooth and well combined.
Step 2: Add the chicken thigh pieces and mix well until fully coated in the marinade. If you have time, let it sit for 1 hour in the fridge—but you can also cook it right away.
Step 3: Thread the chicken onto skewers, packing the pieces snugly but not too tightly.
Step 4: Preheat the air fryer to 400°F. Lightly grease the basket or tray, then arrange the skewers in a single layer, working in batches if needed.
Step 5: Air fry for 12 to 15 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the chicken is cooked through and caramelized on the edges.
Step 6: Serve warm with steamed rice, a sprinkle of sesame seeds, and sliced green onions if desired.

meals.title: Almond Raspberry Scones
meals.description: If you love that buttery, flaky almond croissant vibe but want something a little easier to make at home, these Almond Croissant Raspberry Scones are exactly the answer. They’re tender and flaky with just the right amount of almond flavor, loaded with juicy bursts of raspberries that balance out the richness perfectly. Topped with sliced almonds and a dusting of powdered sugar, they look and taste like something straight from your favorite bakery. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 3 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: ¾ cups cassava flour, additional for dusting surface
egg wash: 1 large egg + 1 tablespoon heavy cream or milk

steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, almond flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar.
Step 2: Grate the chilled butter into the dry ingredients using a box grater. Massage gently with your hands to coat the butter in the flour mixture.
Step 3: In a separate bowl, whisk together the heavy cream, vanilla extract, almond extract, and egg until smooth and combined.
Step 4: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry mixture and stir just until a dough begins to form. Be careful not to overmix.
Step 5: Gently fold in the frozen raspberries, trying not to crush them too much. A little bit of berry juice streaking is fine.
Step 6: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and pat it into a circle about 1 inch thick.

Step 7: Cut the circle into 8 wedges and place them on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Brush the tops of the chilled scones with the egg wash. Press the sliced almonds on top, gently pressing them in so they stick.
Step 8: Chill the scones in the fridge for 20 minutes while you preheat the oven to help the butter in the dough get colder again.
Step 9: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Bake for 18 to 20 minutes, until golden brown and set.
Step 10: Let the scones cool for 5 to 10 minutes on the pan. Dust the tops with powdered sugar and enjoy warm!.

meals.title: Apple Cider Spice Loaf
meals.description: There’s nothing that says fall quite like the smell of apple cider simmering on the stove. It fills the kitchen with that cozy mix of apples, cinnamon, and spice that makes you want to put on a sweater, light a candle, and bake something warm. Living here in Boston, one of my favorite New England weekend activities is going to different farms, trying every apple cider donut I can find, and deciding which one’s the best — it’s serious business at this point. There’s just something about biting into a warm, sugar-dusted donut with crisp air and colorful leaves all around that makes you fall in love with the season all over again. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 5 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: ¼ cup white monk fruit sweetener
½ cup extra virgin olive oil
¾ cup reduced apple cider (start with 1½ cups and simmer 15–20 minutes until it reduces to ¾ cup)
unsalted butter, melted (for brushing after baking)

steps: Step 1: Preheat the oven: Set the oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a loaf pan with parchment paper, leaving some overhang on the sides for easy removal later.

Step 2: Reduce the apple cider: In a small saucepan, simmer 1½ cups apple cider over medium heat for 15–20 minutes until it reduces to about ¾ cup and slightly thickens. Let it cool slightly before using.
Step 3: Make the cinnamon sugar: In a small bowl, mix together the ¼ cup white sugar and ½ teaspoon cinnamon until evenly combined. Set aside.
Step 4: Mix the dry ingredients: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, cardamom, baking soda, baking powder, and salt until evenly combined.
Step 5: Mix the wet ingredients: In a separate bowl, whisk together both sugars, eggs, oil, reduced apple cider, applesauce, and vanilla extract until smooth.
Step 6: Combine the batter: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and whisk until just combined — stop as soon as you no longer see streaks of flour.
Step 7: Pour and top: Pour the batter into the lined loaf pan, spreading it evenly. Sprinkle half of the cinnamon sugar mixture evenly over the top.
Step 8: Bake: Transfer the loaf pan to the oven and bake for 45 to 55 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.

Step 9: Finish with cinnamon sugar: Let the loaf cool for about 10 minutes, then lift it out of the pan and place it on a wire rack. Brush the top with melted butter and sprinkle the remaining cinnamon sugar evenly so it sticks beautifully.
Step 10: Slice and serve: Once fully cooled, cut into thick slices and enjoy!.

meals.title: Classic Apple Hand Pies
meals.description: If you love the apple pie from McDonald’s, you’ll love this simple copycat recipe that you can make at home and even better! The pie is super flaky and crispy while the filling is so ooey and gooey with the most delicious apple spice flavor. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 2 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: granny smith apples, peeled and cubed

steps: Step 1: Whisk together the egg and milk in a small bowl. Set aside.
Step 2: To a small bowl, combine sugar and cinnamon and set aside.
Step 3: To a pan over medium heat, add the butter, apples, light brown sugar, cinnamon, salt, vanilla extract, and lemon juice.
Step 4: Mix together and allow to simmer on low heat for 6 to 8 minutes until the apples are soft when poked with a fork.
Step 5: Combine the water and cornstarch in a small bowl to make the cornstarch slurry and add this to the pan. Mix together to thicken the filling and then take off the heat and transfer to a bowl.
Step 6: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Step 7: Spread the puff pastry on a baking sheet. Cut each sheet into four equal pieces.
Step 8: Brush the edges of each piece with just a bit of egg wash.
Step 9: Add the apple filling along one side and fold over. Press down on the edges to help seal it. Take a fork and press around to seal shut. Repeat with the reset.
Step 10: Brush the tops with egg wash and sprinkle with cinnamon sugar.
Step 11: Transfer to the oven and bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown.
Step 12: Enjoy warm!.

meals.title: Avocado Quinoa Power Salad
meals.description: This Avocado Quinoa Salad is a spot-on copycat of my all-time favorite from Mendocino Farms, where I actually used to work for a summer back in 2015. It’s loaded with crispy quinoa, fresh veggies, and a zesty chipotle vinaigrette that’s just irresistible. I loved it so much, I had to recreate it at home – and now you can too! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps.
steps: Step 1: In a blender or food processor, combine honey, distilled white vinegar, Dijon mustard, a pinch of salt, paprika, adobo chipotle paste, chili powder, neutral oil, and water.
Step 2: Blend until the dressing is well emulsified.
Step 3: Set the dressing aside.
Step 4: Preheat your oven to 400°F (205°C).
Step 5: Spread cooked quinoa on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Step 6: Drizzle avocado oil over the quinoa and toss to coat evenly.
Step 7: Bake in the oven for 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway through, until the quinoa becomes golden and crispy. Keep an eye on it to prevent burning.
Step 8: Once crispy, remove from the oven and let it cool.

Step 9: In a large salad bowl, combine chopped romaine lettuce, chopped kale, diced avocados, diced tomatoes, drained and rinsed black beans, thinly sliced red onion, roasted corn, and chopped fresh cilantro.
Step 10: Toss everything together until well mixed.
Step 11: Once the quinoa has cooled, sprinkle it over the salad for a delightful crunch.
Step 12: Drizzle the chipotle vinaigrette over the salad and toss to coat everything evenly.
Step 13: Finish the salad by sprinkling crumbled cotija cheese on top.
Step 14: Serve immediately and enjoy!.

meals.title: Baked Ziti with Brown Rice Pasta
meals.description: Let’s learn how to make BAKED ZITI, the perfect family dinner everyone will love! The creaminess of the ricotta, the crispy cheese layer on top, and the tangy but sweet pasta sauce turn this into the most memorable dish ever. It’s super easy to make and even prep ahead of time for those busy days. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 1 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: marinara sauce add 6 cups to make it extra saucy

steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 375°F.

Step 2: To a large saucepan over medium-high heat, add the olive oil, ground beef, salt, black pepper, minced garlic, Italian seasoning, and red pepper flakes. As the meat browns, break it up with a wooden spoon, making sure to leave some big chunks too.
Step 3: Once the meat is browned, add in the marinara sauce. Cover and let it simmer on low heat.

Step 4: While the sauce simmers, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and make sure to cook about 2 minutes shy of al dente as the pasta will continue baking in the oven. Drain the pasta.
Step 5: Uncover the sauce and add the heavy cream, chopped basil, and parmesan. Mix all together and combine with the cooked pasta.

Step 6: To a large oven-safe casserole dish, add half of the pasta. Add dollops of ricotta on top. Then sprinkle the mozzarella and parmesan on top. Add the remaining pasta on top and add another layer of dollops of ricotta and cover with mozzarella and parmesan.

Step 7: Transfer to the oven and bake for 20 minutes. Then turn the oven to broil just for a few minutes to crisp up the top. Make sure to watch it closely as it can burn very quickly! Take it out when it's golden and bubbly on top.
Step 8: Enjoy warm!.

meals.title: Creamy Avocado Verde Salsa
meals.description: This copycat Avocado Verde Salsa from Taco Bell is a game-changer, blending the creamy goodness of ripe avocados with the zesty punch of green tomatillos, jalapeños, and a squeeze of fresh lime juice. It’s perfect for topping your tacos, dipping chips, or spreading on your favorite sandwiches, bringing that authentic flavor home. Super quick and easy to whip up, it’s the ultimate way to take your Mexican-inspired dishes to the next level! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps.
steps: Step 1: To a hot pan over medium heat, add the olive oil, tomatillos, jalapeños, and onion. Sear for 4 to 5 minutes and then take off the heat.
Step 2: Add the seared tomatillos, jalapeños, and onions to a food processor along with the garlic cloves, cilantro, avocado, lime juice, water, and salt.
Step 3: Blend until completely smooth.
Step 4: Enjoy on tacos, in a burrito, or with a rice bowl. Transfer to a jar or air-tight container and store in the fridge for 3 to 4 days.

meals.title: Pistachio Baklava Pastries
meals.description: Baklawa meet croissant! Croissant meet Baklawa! This might quite possibly be the best dessert fusion you’ll ever have. Imagine a crispy flakey croissant filled with spiced pistachio filling and orange blossom syrup, and then finished off with crushed pistachios and rose petals! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 2 whole-food ingredient swap(s).
steps: Step 1: To a pot over medium heat, add the water, sugar, and orange blossom water. Bring to a boil so all the sugar is dissolved and then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes. Transfer to a bowl and allow to cool to room temperature so it thickens up.
Step 2: To a bowl, add the pistachio flour, sugar, cornstarch, salt, unsalted butter, vanilla extract, eggs, and heavy cream.
Step 3: Beat together until light and fluffy using a stand or hand mixer.
Step 4: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Step 5: Slice the croissants in half to open them up like a book.
Step 6: Soak both sides in the simple syrup. Be generous with it. You can brush the syrup on or just dunk it for a more efficient method.

Step 7: Add the pistachio filling across the whole inside. Close the croissant. Add a line of pistachio filling across the top and sprinkle on chopped pistachios. Repeat with the rest of the croissants.
Step 8: Bake in the oven for 12 to 15 minutes until crispy all around.
Step 9: Allow to cool for 15 minutes, then dust with powdered sugar, and enjoy!.

meals.title: Banana Bread Oatmeal Bake
meals.description: Did you know you can meal prep oatmeal but make it even better? This chocolate chip banana bread-inspired oatmeal bake has so much texture and flavor. It makes for a delicious breakfast all week! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 1 whole-food ingredient swap(s).
steps: Step 1: Preheat the oven to 350°F.

Step 2: To a large bowl, add the oats, almond milk, mashed bananas, maple syrup, brown sugar, coconut oil, baking powder, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and sea salt. Mix together to combine with a rubber spatula.
Step 3: Line a 9x9 pan with parchment paper. Pour the oat mixture over. Add walnuts and chocolate chips on top.
Step 4: Pop in the oven to bake for 35 to 40 minutes until fully set in the center and crispy on the edges. Do not overbake.
Step 5: Take out of the oven, cool for 10 minutes, then cut into individual slices to enjoy warm!.

meals.title: Banana Bread Pancakes
meals.description: There’s just something about banana pancakes that feels like the ultimate weekend luxury. The smell alone — warm butter, caramelized banana, and melted chocolate — could convince anyone to stay in their pajamas a little longer. These pancakes are soft and fluffy in the center, golden on the edges, and have all the flavors of banana bread but in breakfast form. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 2 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: unsalted butter, melted (plus more for pan)

steps: Step 1: In a large bowl, mash the ripe bananas until mostly smooth with a few small chunks left.
Step 2: Whisk in the egg, melted butter, vanilla extract, and buttermilk until combined.
Step 3: In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, brown sugar, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt.
Step 4: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and use a rubber spatula to gently fold until just combined. Don’t overmix — a few lumps in the batter are perfect.
Step 5: Heat a nonstick pan or griddle over medium heat and add about 1/2 tablespoon of butter to melt.

Step 6: Scoop about 1/2 cup of batter for each pancake, or more depending on how big or small you like them. Once the batter hits the pan, sprinkle a few walnuts and mini chocolate chips right on top before flipping.
Step 7: Cook until bubbles form on top and edges look set, then flip and cook the other side until golden brown.
Step 8: Transfer to a plate and keep warm, then repeat with the remaining batter, adding more butter to the pan as needed.
Step 9: Serve warm with butter, maple syrup, and an extra sprinkle of chocolate chips.

meals.title: Banana Cake with Brown Butter Frosting
meals.description: The most moist banana cake you’ll ever have and because it has bananas in it, it can even be breakfast! It’s finished off with a brown butter cream cheese frosting that makes this dessert irresistible! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 4 whole-food ingredient swap(s).
steps: Step 1: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Step 2: To a large mixing bowl, add the butter, light brown sugar, and white sugar. Use a mixer to beat the sugar and butter together until light and fluffy.
Step 3: To the same bowl with the butter and sugar, add the eggs, smashed banana, vanilla, and milk. Whisk together again until smooth.
Step 4: Now add all the dry ingredients to the wet. This includes all-purpose flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
Step 5: Combine together until you have a batter.
Step 6: Line an 8 x 8 pan with parchment paper and transfer the batter.
Step 7: Transfer to the oven and bake for 25 to 28 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean in the center.
Step 8: Remove from the oven and allow to cool for about an hour before frosting.
Step 9: Start by browning the butter.

Step 10: To a pan over medium heat, add the butter. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes until it starts to bubble and then brown. Make sure not to cook it any further or it can burn. Transfer it to a bowl and allow it to cool so it's not hot. You can transfer it to the fridge for a few minutes until it sets solid at room temperature.
Step 11: To a mixing bowl, add the browned butter, cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla extract.
Step 12: Beat for 1 to 2 minutes until fluffy and smooth.
Step 13: Once the cake is cooled, add the frosting on top, adding as little or as much as you'd like.
Step 14: Slice and enjoy!.

meals.title: Banana French Toast Muffins
meals.description: These banana French toast muffins taste just like banana bread, but in soft, golden, perfectly portioned muffin form. They’re great for meal prep, freeze easily, and reheat beautifully for busy mornings. Enjoy them warm with maple syrup and/or powdered sugar for the perfect finishing touch. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 2 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: brioche bread, cut into small cubes
monk fruit powdered sweetener, for serving

steps: Step 1: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a muffin tin with liners. If you don’t have liners, grease the muffin tin generously with melted unsalted butter.
Step 2: In a large bowl, mash the bananas until broken down but still slightly chunky — some banana pieces are fine.
Step 3: Add the half-and-half, eggs, brown sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg to the bowl and whisk until combined.
Step 4: Add the brioche cubes and gently toss until evenly coated in the custard.
Step 5: Fold in the chocolate chips and chopped walnuts.
Step 6: Divide the mixture evenly between the muffin liners, pressing down gently so the bread is compact.
Step 7: Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, until puffed and golden brown on top.
Step 8: Let cool slightly before removing from the pan, and enjoy warm with maple syrup and/or powdered sugar.

meals.title: Creamy Banana Milk
meals.description: With just four ingredients and five minutes, you can make your own batch of banana milk at home! And if you haven’t tried banana milk yet, you’re seriously missing out on this super popular drink in South Korea. It’s creamy, naturally sweet, and absolutely delicious on its own or as a dreamy addition to your coffee or matcha. Trust me, once you make it, you’ll wonder how you ever lived without it! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 1 whole-food ingredient swap(s).
steps: Step 1: In a blender, combine the whole milk, bananas, vanilla extract, sugar, and a pinch of salt.
Step 2: Blend the mixture on high speed until smooth and well combined.
Step 3: Place a cheese cloth over a large bowl or pitcher and pour the blended mixture through the cloth to strain it. This will ensure a silky texture by removing any banana pulp.
Step 4: Transfer the strained banana milk to a jug or container and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Step 5: Give the banana milk a good stir before pouring it into glasses. Enjoy it on its own or in some coffee or matcha!.

meals.title: Bang Bang Chicken Skewers
meals.description: Bang Bang Chicken Skewers in the air fryer and in under 30 minutes! This easy and healthy dinner packs so much flavor from the creamy bang bang sauce that’s a bit spicy and a bit sweet. Serve it with rice and some steamed broccoli for the complete meal! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 2 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: red pepper flakes, optional for more spice

steps: Step 1: Preheat the air fryer or oven to 450°F.
Step 2: In a small bowl, make the bang bang sauce. Combine mayonnaise, sweet chili sauce, garlic, rice vinegar, sriracha, and parsley until smooth. Set aside.

Step 3: To a bowl, add the chopped chicken along with soy sauce, olive oil, oyster sauce, garlic, ginger, chili powder, smoked paprika, onion powder, salt, black pepper, and light brown sugar. Mix together and if you have the time, let it marinate for 30 minutes.

Step 4: After marinating, thread the chicken pieces onto skewers. If using wooden skewers, make sure they have soaked in water for 30 minutes to prevent them from burning. I like to add about 6 to 7 pieces onto each skewer.
Step 5: Add the skewers to the air fryer and air fry for about 13 to 15 minutes at 450°F until fully cooked and charred.
Step 6: Drizzle bang bang sauce on top and add green onions to enjoy.

meals.title: Beef and Cheese Borek Rolls
meals.description: Flavor-first recipe upgraded for clean whole-food eating. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 1 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: 2 cups crumbled ackawi cheese more or less to taste
2 cups shredded mozzarella more or less to taste
extra virgin olive oil for frying
optional: avocado oil spray for air frying

steps: Step 1: Heat a pan over medium heat and add olive oil and garlic. Sauce for a minute.
Step 2: Add ground beef along with paprika, allspice, coriander, salt, cumin, black pepper, and turmeric.
Step 3: Start to break up the ground beef with a spatula or wooden spoon.
Step 4: Once the meat is browned and minced finely, turn off the heat and add parsley, cilantro, and chopped tomatoes. Mix and transfer to a bowl to cool to room temperature.
Step 5: When the ground beef is at room temperature, add the ackawi cheese and mozzarella. Mix.
Step 6: Grab an egg roll wrapper. Add about 2 tablespoons of filling along one edge.
Step 7: Fold the left and right side in toward the center first and then roll until you get almost to the end. Brush the end with water and finish the last roll to seal tightly.

Step 8: TO PAN FRY: Heat oil for frying in a pan. Drop the egg rolls in and don't overcrowd. Once golden brown on one side, about 2 to 3 minutes, flip over and allow the other side to get golden brown. Transfer to a plate lined with a paper towel to drain any excess oil.

Step 9: TO AIR FRY: Set the air fryer to 375°F. Spray the basket and egg rolls with avocado oil or vegetable oil spray. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes, then flip over and cook another 5 to 6 minutes or until golden and crispy all around.
Step 10: Enjoy warm with your favorite dipping sauce!.

meals.title: Beef Chorizo and Chicken Pasta
meals.description: Creamy pasta with beef chorizo AND chicken – double the protein, double the flavor! The best way to describe this pasta is flavorful. The beef chorizo adds so much spice to the sauce so you don’t even need to add much to the pasta for it to be the best you’ve ever had! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 1 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: freshly grated parmesan, more to taste
rigatoni brown rice + quinoa pasta or penne

steps: Step 1: In a bowl, add the chicken, olive oil, salt, paprika, garlic powder, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and oregano. Mix to marinade evenly.
Step 2: Heat an oiled skillet or pan over medium-high heat.

Step 3: Add the marinated chicken to the pan and cook on each side until golden brown, about 3 minutes on each side. Then drop the heat to low and allow the chicken to cook through to the center or the temperature registers 165°F. Remove the chicken from the pan.
Step 4: Allow the chicken to cool for 5 to 10 minutes and then chop into small pieces. Set aside.
Step 5: Cook pasta according to package directions in salted water.
Step 6: Drain the pasta after cooked.
Step 7: In a saucepan over medium heat, add the beef chorizo. Cook for 6 to 7 minutes until browned. Transfer the chorizo to a plate, leaving a bit of the oil in the pan.
Step 8: In the same pan the chorizo was cooked in, melt down the butter and cook the garlic and tomato paste for 1 to 2 minutes.
Step 9: Then add the heavy cream, cream cheese, chili powder, dried basil, parmesan, and the cooked chorizo. Whisk together and bring to a simmer for a few minutes to thicken.
Step 10: Once it thickens, take off the heat and add back the pasta and parsley.
Step 11: Mix together to combine and then enjoy!.

meals.title: Slow-Simmered Beef Bone Broth
meals.description: There’s nothing like a cozy, warm bowl of beef bone broth, especially when the weather starts to cool down. This homemade version has all the flavor and goodness you’ll ever need, and it’s so much better than anything you’ll find on store shelves! Roasting the bones and veggies before simmering them adds layers of deep, rich flavor, and with warming spices like cinnamon and turmeric, this broth is perfect for sipping straight or using as a base for soups, stews, or even sauces. Let’s dive into how to make this cozy, nutrient-packed beef bone broth in the Instant Pot! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps.

ingredients.name: beef bones (marrow bones, knuckles, or oxtails)
12 cups water (enough to cover the bones)

steps: Step 1: Pre-Boil the Bones (Optional but Recommended): Place the beef bones in a large pot and cover them with water. Bring the water to a boil over high heat, and let the bones boil for 10-15 minutes. Drain and rinse the bones under cold water to remove impurities.

Step 2: Roast the Bones and Vegetables: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). On a large baking sheet, arrange the pre-boiled bones along with the chopped celery, carrots, and quartered onions. Roast everything for 45 to 55 minutes, until the bones and vegetables are deeply browned. This step enhances the flavor of your broth.

Step 3: Prepare the Instant Pot: Transfer the roasted bones and vegetables to the Instant Pot. Add the garlic, thyme sprigs, bay leaves, peppercorns, cinnamon sticks, turmeric, salt, white pepper, and cayenne powder. Pour in the apple cider vinegar.
Step 4: Add Water: Pour enough water into the Instant Pot to fully cover the bones and vegetables, making sure not to exceed the "Max Fill" line (about 10-12 cups of water).
Step 5: Pressure Cook: Secure the Instant Pot lid and set the valve to "Sealing." Cook on High Pressure for 2 hours (or up to 4 hours for a more intense broth).
Step 6: Natural Release: After the cooking cycle is finished, allow the pressure to release naturally for about 20-30 minutes. Carefully open the lid once the pressure is fully released.
Step 7: Strain the Broth: Using a fine-mesh strainer, strain the broth into a large pot or container, discarding the bones and vegetables (or saving any bits of meat if desired).

Step 8: Cool and Store: Let the broth cool, then refrigerate overnight. Skim off any solidified fat that forms on top. Store the broth in jars or containers in the fridge for up to 5 days, or freeze for longer storage.

meals.title: Beef and Lamb Sausage Rolls
meals.description: Sausage rolls are a staple in the UK — something you’ll see in bakeries, cafés, grocery stores, and even train stations — and they’re popular for a reason. Flaky pastry wrapped around a savory filling is hard to beat, but I’ve always wanted to make my own version at home. The meat filling in this recipe is inspired by Bosnian ćevapi, using a beef-and-lamb mixture that stays incredibly juicy and tender inside crisp, golden puff pastry. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps.
steps: Step 1: Make the filling: In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, ground lamb, garlic, puréed onion, smoked paprika, salt, baking soda, black pepper, cayenne, and egg. Mix until fully combined and smooth, without overmixing.
Step 2: Prep the pastry: Lightly flour a work surface and roll out each puff pastry sheet just enough to smooth it out. Cut each sheet in half lengthwise to create four long rectangles.
Step 3: Egg wash the pastry: Whisk together the egg and heavy cream. Brush egg wash along both long sides of each puff pastry rectangle.

Step 4: Pipe the filling: Transfer the sausage mixture to a piping bag (or zip-top bag with the corner cut). Pipe a thick strip of filling slightly off-center, closer to one long edge, leaving enough pastry to fold over.

Step 5: Fold, trim, and seal: Fold the pastry over the filling and press down right where the meat ends. A little excess pastry is fine, but if there’s too much overlap, trim some off so it doesn’t get doughy. Use a fork to press and seal the edge tightly.

Step 6: Slice and finish: Cut each long strip into 1½- to 2-inch pieces. Arrange on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Brush the tops with more egg wash and make 2 slits on the top of each one, not cutting all the way down but just to let some steam out as they cook. Then sprinkle the tops with sesame seeds.
Step 7: Bake: Bake at 375°F for 25 to 30 minutes, until the pastry is deeply golden and puffed and the filling is cooked through.
Step 8: Cool and serve: Let cool slightly before serving.

meals.title: Cassava Flour Beignets
meals.description: Transport yourself to the magical streets of New Orleans with these fluffy pillow-like Beignets! Inspired by The Princess and the Frog movie and my own trip to Cafe du Monde, these beignets packed with a mountain top of powdered sugar will leave you speechless with every bite. Not to mention, they’re shockingly so easy to make from scratch! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 4 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil, for frying
monk fruit powdered sweetener, for dusting

steps: Step 1: In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm milk and active dry yeast. Whisk together until the yeast is dissolved. Allow it to sit for about 5-10 minutes until it becomes frothy.
Step 2: To the yeast mixture, add the granulated white sugar, salt, eggs, softened unsalted butter, and vanilla extract. Mix well until all the ingredients are combined.
Step 3: Gradually add the all-purpose flour to the mixture, stirring continuously until a dough forms. It will be very soft and a bit sticky.
Step 4: Knead the dough for about 3 to 4 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. You can use your hand, a wooden spatula, or a stand mixer with the hook attachment.

Step 5: Place the kneaded dough into a lightly greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap and a clean kitchen towel, and let it rise in a warm place for about 1 to 1 1/2 hours, or until it doubles in size.
Step 6: Once the dough has risen, punch it down to release the air. Transfer it to a clean and floured surface.
Step 7: Roll out the dough to about 1/2 inch thickness into a large square. Use a pizza cutter to cut squares about the size of 2.5 by 2.5 inches.
Step 8: Cover them with a clean kitchen towel and let them rise again but this time only for about 15 to 20 minutes, just while the oil heats up.
Step 9: In a deep pot or fryer pan, heat vegetable oil to 350°F (175°C).
Step 10: Carefully add a few donuts to the hot oil, making sure not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for about 1-2 minutes on each side, or until they turn golden brown.
Step 11: Once fried, remove the donuts from the oil using a slotted spoon and place them on a wire rack to drain any excess oil.
Step 12: Repeat the frying process with the remaining donuts.
Step 13: Once all the donuts are fried and cooled slightly, dust them very generously with powdered sugar to enjoy warm.

meals.title: Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry
meals.description: If you’re craving classic Chinese takeout, this beef and broccoli stir fry is about to become your new favorite. It’s everything you love about takeout — tender, melt-in-your-mouth steak paired with crisp broccoli — but made at home and even better. The sauce is perfectly balanced: savory with a touch of sweetness and just a hint of heat, clinging to every bite. Plus, it all comes together in one pan in under 30 minutes, making it a fantastic weeknight dinner when time is tight but you still want something special. Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 4 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: flank, NY strip, or ribeye steak, thinly sliced against the grain

steps: Step 1: Marinate the steak: In a medium bowl, toss the sliced steak with cornstarch, dark soy sauce, baking soda, white pepper, and salt. Set aside.

Step 2: Prepare the sauce: In another bowl or measuring cup, whisk beef broth, low sodium soy sauce, brown sugar, oyster sauce, rice vinegar, dark soy sauce, cornstarch, white pepper, and cayenne powder together until smooth. Set aside.

Step 3: Cook the broccoli: Heat 1 tablespoon neutral oil in a large pan or wok over medium heat. Add broccoli and stir-fry briefly. Add a splash of water, cover, and steam for 2-3 minutes until broccoli is tender-crisp. Remove broccoli and set aside.

Step 4: Cook the steak: Over medium-high heat, heat the wok or pan until it’s very hot, then add 1 1/2 to 2 tablespoons neutral oil. Add the marinated steak and stir-fry until just cooked through and seared, about 2-3 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Step 5: Sauté aromatics: Lower the heat to medium-low. Add sesame oil and 1/2 tablespoon neutral oil to the pan. Add garlic and ginger, sauté gently until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Step 6: Combine: Return broccoli and steak to the pan. Pour in the sauce and toss everything together until the sauce thickens and coats evenly, about 1-2 minutes.
Step 7: Serve hot with rice or noodles.

meals.title: Homestyle Smash Burger
meals.description: Making a Big Mac at home and even better is easier than you think! Two patties sandwiched between THREE BUNS and layers of Big Mac sauce, lettuce, onions, pickles, and cheese – what could truly be better? It’s juicy, saucy, comforting, and everything you want in the perfect burger! Crafted with Real-Food standards: no seed oils, no refined sugar, gluten-smart swaps. Includes 2 whole-food ingredient swap(s).

ingredients.name: buns, plus additional bottom buns for the middle sourdough bun
extra virgin olive oil, split in half

steps: Step 1: In a small bowl, mix together the mayonnaise, relish, ketchup, sugar, and vinegar. Set aside.

Step 2: Weigh out 8 portions of 3.5 oz of ground beef or eye it out. Gently flatten the patty into a circle to desired thin or thickness, making sure to not mix or mold it too much. Sprinkle with salt and pepper on both sides. Set aside.
Step 3: Brush the insides of the buns with butter.
Step 4: Heat a pan or skillet over medium heat. Lay the buns butter side face down. Take them off the pan when a nice golden brown develops around the edges.

Step 5: Over medium-high heat in a skillet or pan, add 1/2 a tablespoon of butter and 1/2 a tablespoon of vegetable oil. Add the patties in, two at a time or more if you have a large grill/skillet. Add a teaspoon of mustard on the raw side and spread evenly. It will take about 2-3 minutes for the patty to get a nice brown crust. Flip over and add a slice of American cheese on top of half the patties you cook since Big Macs only have 1 cheese but 2 patties. Let it cook for about 1 more minute for the cheese to melt. Take off the heat.
Step 6: To the bottom bun, add sauce, lettuce, onion, patty, and cheese. Then add the middle bun, sauce, lettuce, onion, pickles, and the second patty. Add the top bun.
Step 7: Enjoy warm!.

meals.description: Flavor-forward meal reworked for clean whole-food cooking. Built with smarter whole-food swaps while keeping the original flavor profile.

sourceURL=core-block-supports-inline-css */ Moribyan Search Moribyan Recipes
I prep proteins in advance so meals come together fast and I don’t have to handle prepping any raw meat
I keep flavors bold so nothing tastes dull after a few days
I prep sauces separately to avoid repetition
I choose recipes that reheat well or taste great cold
You can bake, grill, air-fry, or pan-sear them
They freeze beautifully in their marinades
One batch of chicken can turn into multiple meals
Serve with rice, quinoa, or orzo in a bowl
Serve with brown rice noodles or a brown rice + quinoa pasta
Flake into salads the next day
Pair with rice and a sauce
Steak bowls with rice and veggies
Added to salads for extra protein
With any kind of potatoes and roasted veggies!
Prep Sauces: These are sauces that you dress a bowl with or use as a spread in a sandwich or wrap
Simmer Sauces: These are sauces that you cook into so think of butter chicken, red curry, alfredo, and creamy “vodka” sauce.
Use instead of bottled salad dressing
Add brown rice + quinoa pasta or protein into the simmer sauces
Salad Three Ways: Egg, Chicken, or Tuna
Beef and Cheese Börek Egg Rolls
Arrange the egg rolls in a single layer on a sheet pan
Freeze until solid so they don’t stick together
Transfer to a zip-top bag and store in the freezer

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
20 drops lavender or santal essential oil
1/3 cup castile soap, add as needed
Add water, white vinegar, castile soap, and the essential oil to the spray bottle.
Seal the bottle and shake it around to combine all the ingredients together.
Spray on countertops and wipe with a microfiber or paper towel to clean!
To a bowl, combine baking soda with soap and essential oil until you have a thick but smooth paste.
Transfer the paste to an airtight jar and keep under your sink.
Whenever you need it, add a spoonful to your sink and use a sponge or sink brush to scrub clean!
To a bowl, mix together baking soda with lemon and orange zest.
Layer the baking soda in a jar with lemon/orange wedge peels to create about 3 layers.
Place the jar open in the fridge and it lasts about 2 weeks!

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Serve grilled or baked chicken over couscous or rice with a big green salad.
Slice the cooked chicken and stuff it into pita pockets with garlic sauce and pickled onions.
Roast the chicken with potatoes for a cozy, flavorful dinner.
Fill pita or flatbreads with the cooked chicken, garlic sauce, and fresh parsley for a street-food vibe.
Toss into a salad with cucumbers, tomatoes, and tahini dressing.
Serve over turmeric rice with roasted veggies and a dollop of yogurt.
Bake or air fry and serve with jasmine rice and sautéed green beans.
Chop cooked chicken into a cold noodle salad with sesame dressing.
Top a bowl of ramen or miso soup with sliced chicken.
Grill with charred corn, avocado, and black bean salad.
Shred for tacos or burrito bowls.
Roast with potatoes and peppers for a one-pan meal.
Stir-fry and serve with jasmine rice and steamed veggies.
Simmer with extra coconut milk and vegetables for a green curry stew.
Top rice noodle bowls with fresh herbs and crushed peanuts.

Recipes
Tastes just like a classic apple cider donut, but in loaf form.
No frying or mess — just whisk, bake, and slice.
That buttery cinnamon sugar topping gives the perfect crunch.
Uses reduced apple cider for that deep, real apple flavor.
Makes your kitchen smell like a cozy fall day in New England.
A bit of cream or milk
Don’t skip reducing the apple cider — it gives the loaf its deep, cozy flavor.
Use unsweetened applesauce so the loaf isn’t overly sweet.
Brush the loaf while it’s still slightly warm so the sugar sticks perfectly.
Don’t overmix the batter — it keeps the crumb soft and fluffy.
Store covered at room temperature for up to 3 days or in the fridge for up to a week.
Warm slices in the microwave or toaster oven for 20 to 25 seconds for that fresh-from-the-oven taste.
Stir in chopped apples or toasted pecans for a little texture.
Swap the glaze for a drizzle of caramel for an indulgent twist.
Toast a slice in a pan with some butter on both sides like you would with banana bread for a crisp exterior!
Or keep it simple — just a warm slice with your morning coffee is perfect on its own.
Pumpkin Pie French Toast — thick, custardy, and tastes just like a slice of pumpkin pie for breakfast.
Pumpkin Churros — golden, pumpkin-shaped churros coated in warm cinnamon sugar.
Pumpkin Sticky Toffee Pudding — rich, cozy, and drenched in buttery toffee sauce.
Pumpkin Mac and Cheese — creamy, savory, and the ultimate cozy dinner to balance out all the sweets.
Pumpkin Cream Cheese Danishes — flaky, buttery pastry filled with a spiced pumpkin and cream cheese swirl.
¼ cup white monk fruit sweetener
½ cup extra virgin olive oil
¾ cup reduced apple cider (start with 1½ cups and simmer 15–20 minutes until it reduces to ¾ cup)
unsalted butter, melted (for brushing after baking)
Preheat the oven: Set the oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a loaf pan with parchment paper, leaving some overhang on the sides for easy removal later.
Reduce the apple cider: In a small saucepan, simmer 1½ cups apple cider over medium heat for 15–20 minutes until it reduces to about ¾ cup and slightly thickens. Let it cool slightly before using.
Make the cinnamon sugar: In a small bowl, mix together the ¼ cup monk fruit sweetener and ½ teaspoon cinnamon until evenly combined. Set aside.
Mix the dry ingredients: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, cardamom, baking soda, baking powder, and salt until evenly combined.
Mix the wet ingredients: In a separate bowl, whisk together both sugars, eggs, oil, reduced apple cider, applesauce, and vanilla extract until smooth.
Combine the batter: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and whisk until just combined — stop as soon as you no longer see streaks of flour.
Pour and top: Pour the batter into the lined loaf pan, spreading it evenly. Sprinkle half of the cinnamon sugar mixture evenly over the top.
Bake: Transfer the loaf pan to the oven and bake for 45 to 55 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.

Finish with cinnamon sugar: Let the loaf cool for about 10 minutes, then lift it out of the pan and place it on a wire rack. Brush the top with melted butter and sprinkle the remaining cinnamon sugar evenly so it sticks beautifully.
Slice and serve: Once fully cooled, cut into thick slices and enjoy!

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

meals.title: Clean-Plate Apple Pie – McDonald’s Copycat
meals.description: Flavor-forward meal reworked for clean whole-food cooking. Built with smarter whole-food swaps while keeping the original flavor profile.

Recipes
It needs to have the perfect balance of sweet and tart. It can’t be too sour or too sweet – just right!
The apples need to be cooked perfectly. You don’t want the apples to be undercooked and still crunchy but you also don’t want them to be overcooked and be too mushy.
Just combine together an egg and a splash of milk in a small bowl. That’s it!
To a small bowl, add monk fruit sweetener and cinnamon and mix together. Set aside.
granny smith apples, peeled and cubed
Whisk together the egg and milk in a small bowl. Set aside.
To a small bowl, combine sugar and cinnamon and set aside.
To a pan over medium heat, add the butter, apples, light coconut sugar, cinnamon, salt, vanilla extract, and lemon juice.
Mix together and allow to simmer on low heat for 6 to 8 minutes until the apples are soft when poked with a fork.
Combine the water and cornstarch in a small bowl to make the cornstarch slurry and add this to the pan. Mix together to thicken the filling and then take off the heat and transfer to a bowl.
Spread the puff pastry on a baking sheet. Cut each sheet into four equal pieces.
Brush the edges of each piece with just a bit of egg wash.
Add the apple filling along one side and fold over. Press down on the edges to help seal it. Take a fork and press around to seal shut. Repeat with the reset.
Brush the tops with egg wash and sprinkle with cinnamon sugar.
Transfer to the oven and bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes

Protein: To make this salad more substantial, add some grilled chicken, shrimp, or even tofu. The flavors pair wonderfully with the chipotle vinaigrette. You could also make this a copycat recipe of Mendo’s Impossible Taco Salad which is this same salad but instead of chicken, add impossible plant-based chorizo meat and instead of this dressing, it’s a vegan chipotle ranch.
Beans: If you’re not a fan of black beans, swap them out for chickpeas or pinto beans.
Jicama: The real Mendocino Farms salad actually has jicama in it but I opted out.
Greens: If you’re not into kale, just use all romaine or mix in some spinach.
Vinaigrette: If you have extra dressing, it keeps well in the fridge for up to a week. It’s great on other salads or even as a marinade for proteins.
In a blender or food processor, combine honey, distilled white vinegar, Dijon mustard, a pinch of salt, paprika, adobo chipotle paste, chili powder, neutral oil, and water.
Blend until the dressing is well emulsified.
Spread cooked quinoa on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle avocado oil over the quinoa and toss to coat evenly.
Bake in the oven for 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway through, until the quinoa becomes golden and crispy. Keep an eye on it to prevent burning.
Once crispy, remove from the oven and let it cool.

In a large salad bowl, combine chopped romaine lettuce, chopped kale, diced avocados, diced tomatoes, drained and rinsed black beans, thinly sliced red onion, roasted corn, and chopped fresh cilantro.
Toss everything together until well mixed.
Once the quinoa has cooled, sprinkle it over the salad for a delightful crunch.
Drizzle the chipotle vinaigrette over the salad and toss to coat everything evenly.
Finish the salad by sprinkling crumbled cotija cheese on top.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
To a hot pan over medium heat, add the olive oil, tomatillos, jalapeños, and onion. Sear for 4 to 5 minutes and then take off the heat.
Add the seared tomatillos, jalapeños, and onions to a food processor along with the garlic cloves, cilantro, avocado, lime juice, water, and salt.
Enjoy on tacos, in a burrito, or with a rice bowl. Transfer to a jar or air-tight container and store in the fridge for 3 to 4 days.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

meals.description: Flavor-forward meal reworked for clean whole-food cooking. Built with smarter whole-food swaps while keeping the original flavor profile.

Recipes
In a large oven safe baking dish, add the cherry tomatoes and in the center, add your block of feta cheese.
Drizzle olive oil on top along with oregano, parsley, salt, balsamic vinegar, black pepper, and red pepper flakes. Mix to coat evenly.
Bake in the oven for 35 minutes.
While it bakes, add your brown rice + quinoa pasta to salted boiling water and cook to package directions.
Take out of the oven and add the minced garlic. Break apart the feta and mix with the tomatoes and garlic.
Add the cooked brown rice + quinoa pasta, ricotta, roasted red bell pepper, and chopped basil. Mix together.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper to desired level.
Grate fresh parmesan on top and enjoy!
Other veggies that you can incorporate into this dish include sun dried tomatoes, zucchini, onions, olives, and mushrooms.
You can use any brown rice + quinoa pasta shape you like! I used Orecchiette and highly recommend it.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
sheets, cut each sheet into 4 squares
cooked sushi rice or sticky Jasmine rice
To make the spicy mayo, mix together mayo, sambal, sriracha, sesame oil, sugar, and lemon juice in a small bowl. Once it's creamy and smooth, set aside.
To make the salmon, cube it into small individual bites first.
Add mayo, sriracha, lime juice, sesame oil, low sodium coconut aminos, and chili powder. Mix together until marinaded evenly. Set aside.
Add 1 to 2 tablespoons of sticky rice to each nori square. Spread flat. Press into the cupcake tin until the tray is full.
Add about 1 to 2 tablespoons of the salmon on top.
Bake in the oven for 15 minutes. Optional, turn to broil for 2 minutes, give or take, until the top is crispy and golden.
Take out of the oven and pick your favorite toppings to add on top before enjoying!

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
Mozzarella: You can use pre-grated mozzarella in this recipe but highly recommend grating it yourself.
Parmesan: Try to buy the highest quality of parmesan you can find. It truly makes all the difference in flavor when you grate it yourself! Don’t use the powder kind.
Ricotta: Also buy the highest quality of ricotta you can find. A lot of the cheaper ones tend to be more watery and less creamy.
Cajun Chicken brown rice + quinoa pasta
Cheesecake Factory Copycat Chipotle Chicken brown rice + quinoa pasta
marinara sauce add 6 cups to make it extra saucy

To a large saucepan over medium-high heat, add the olive oil, ground beef, salt, black pepper, minced garlic, Italian seasoning, and red pepper flakes. As the meat browns, break it up with a wooden spoon, making sure to leave some big chunks too.
Once the meat is browned, add in the marinara sauce. Cover and let it simmer on low heat.

While the sauce simmers, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the brown rice + quinoa pasta and make sure to cook about 2 minutes shy of al dente as the brown rice + quinoa pasta will continue baking in the oven. Drain the brown rice + quinoa pasta.
Uncover the sauce and add the heavy cream, chopped basil, and parmesan. Mix all together and combine with the cooked brown rice + quinoa pasta.

To a large oven-safe casserole dish, add half of the brown rice + quinoa pasta. Add dollops of ricotta on top. Then sprinkle the mozzarella and parmesan on top. Add the remaining brown rice + quinoa pasta on top and add another layer of dollops of ricotta and cover with mozzarella and parmesan.

Transfer to the oven and bake for 20 minutes. Then turn the oven to broil just for a few minutes to crisp up the top. Make sure to watch it closely as it can burn very quickly! Take it out when it's golden and bubbly on top.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
To a large mixing bowl, add the butter, light coconut sugar, and monk fruit sweetener. Use a mixer to beat the sugar and butter together until light and fluffy.
To the same bowl with the butter and sugar, add the eggs, smashed banana, vanilla, and milk. Whisk together again until smooth.
Now add all the dry ingredients to the wet. This includes cassava flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
Combine together until you have a batter.
Line an 8 x 8 pan with parchment paper and transfer the batter.
Transfer to the oven and bake for 25 to 28 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean in the center.
Remove from the oven and allow to cool for about an hour before frosting.

To a pan over medium heat, add the butter. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes until it starts to bubble and then brown. Make sure not to cook it any further or it can burn. Transfer it to a bowl and allow it to cool so it's not hot. You can transfer it to the fridge for a few minutes until it sets solid at room temperature.
To a mixing bowl, add the browned butter, cream cheese, monk fruit powdered sweetener, and vanilla extract.
Beat for 1 to 2 minutes until fluffy and smooth.
Once the cake is cooled, add the frosting on top, adding as little or as much as you'd like.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

meals.title: Clean-Plate Banana French Toast Muffins
meals.description: Flavor-forward meal reworked for clean whole-food cooking. Built with smarter whole-food swaps while keeping the original flavor profile.

Recipes
It tastes like banana bread and French toast combined
Everything gets mixed in one bowl
No standing at the stove flipping French toast
Perfect for meal prep and freezing
Great for breakfast, brunch, or a snack on the go
The muffins bake up soft inside with crisp edges
And it comes together way more quickly!
Brioche bread for richness and structure
Cinnamon and nutmeg for classic banana bread flavor
Bananas, smashed with some chunks left
Chocolate chips and walnuts for texture and sweetness
From the fridge: Microwave for 20 to 30 seconds
From frozen: Microwave for 45 to 60 seconds
Oven method: Reheat at 300°F for 8 to 10 minutes if refrigerated, or 12 to 15 minutes if frozen
brioche bread, cut into small cubes
monk fruit powdered sweetener, for serving
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a muffin tin with liners. If you don’t have liners, grease the muffin tin generously with melted unsalted butter.
In a large bowl, mash the bananas until broken down but still slightly chunky — some banana pieces are fine.
Add the half-and-half, eggs, coconut sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and nutmeg to the bowl and whisk until combined.
Add the brioche cubes and gently toss until evenly coated in the custard.
Fold in the chocolate chips and chopped walnuts.
Divide the mixture evenly between the muffin liners, pressing down gently so the bread is compact.
Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, until puffed and golden brown on top.
Let cool slightly before removing from the pan, and enjoy warm with maple syrup and/or monk fruit powdered sweetener.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
extra virgin olive oil for frying
To a saucepan, add the butter and olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and tomato paste and cook for about 4 minutes.
The tomato paste will darken in color from a bright red to reddish brown. This process removes acidity from the tomato paste.
Then add the red pepper flakes, black pepper, paprika, cumin, and salt. Stir together.
Finally, finish it off with cilantro and lemon juice and mix together until a sauce forms.
Taste and adjust anything to your liking. If you want it more spicy, add more red pepper flakes or I like to add sriracha. Then set aside.
Add the potatoes to ice cold water and allow to soak for 10 minutes.
To a pot of boiling salted water, add the cubed potatoes and parboil for 5 to 6 minutes. Drain all the water and pat dry.
Toss the potatoes in cornstarch until all are coated evenly.

Heat a pan with extra virgin olive oil for frying (about 350°F). Once hot, add the potatoes, making sure they're not overcrowded. Fry until golden brown and then transfer to the pan with the tomato sauce.
Toss the potatoes in the sauce and serve warm!

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
Cajun Chicken brown rice + quinoa pasta
Gigi Hadid brown rice + quinoa pasta
Sun Dried Tomato brown rice + quinoa pasta
freshly grated parmesan, more to taste
rigatoni brown rice + quinoa pasta or penne
In a bowl, add the chicken, olive oil, salt, paprika, garlic powder, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and oregano. Mix to marinade evenly.
Heat an oiled skillet or pan over medium-high heat.

Add the marinated chicken to the pan and cook on each side until golden brown, about 3 minutes on each side. Then drop the heat to low and allow the chicken to cook through to the center or the temperature registers 165°F. Remove the chicken from the pan.
Allow the chicken to cool for 5 to 10 minutes and then chop into small pieces. Set aside.
Cook brown rice + quinoa pasta according to package directions in salted water.
Drain the brown rice + quinoa pasta after cooked.
In a saucepan over medium heat, add the beef chorizo. Cook for 6 to 7 minutes until browned. Transfer the chorizo to a plate, leaving a bit of the oil in the pan.
In the same pan the chorizo was cooked in, melt down the butter and cook the garlic and tomato paste for 1 to 2 minutes.
Then add the heavy cream, cream cheese, chili powder, dried basil, parmesan, and the cooked chorizo. Whisk together and bring to a simmer for a few minutes to thicken.
Once it thickens, take off the heat and add back the brown rice + quinoa pasta and parsley.
Mix together to combine and then enjoy!

There is no salt in the sauce recipe if you noticed! This is because the chorizo is already very salty so when it’s mixed into the sauce along with the parmesan, the sauce naturally gets salted too. Of course, taste it and adjust it as needed for your taste.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
Puréed or finely grated onion for moisture and flavor
Baking soda to keep the meat soft, springy, and juicy
Press firmly where the meat ends
Keep the seam flat, not tucked underneath
Use a fork to seal the edge tightly along the entire length
Deep golden tops with visible puff
Crisp bottoms that release easily from the parchment
No visible raw filling at the seams
Use all beef if you don’t have lamb
Skip sesame seeds or swap for nigella seeds or a blend of both
Make them slightly larger for bakery-style rolls or smaller for parties
Assemble the rolls completely but do not egg wash the tops
Arrange them on a parchment-lined sheet pan in a single layer, leaving space so they don’t touch
Transfer the rolls to a zip-top freezer bag
Label and store for up to 3 months
Place frozen rolls on a baking sheet
Brush with egg wash and sprinkle with sesame seeds
Bake at 375°F, adding a few extra minutes to the bake time
Store in the fridge for up to 24 hours
Egg wash and bake just before serving

Make the filling: In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, ground lamb, garlic, puréed onion, smoked paprika, salt, baking soda, black pepper, cayenne, and egg. Mix until fully combined and smooth, without overmixing.
Prep the pastry: Lightly flour a work surface and roll out each puff pastry sheet just enough to smooth it out. Cut each sheet in half lengthwise to create four long rectangles.
Egg wash the pastry: Whisk together the egg and heavy cream. Brush egg wash along both long sides of each puff pastry rectangle.

Pipe the filling: Transfer the sausage mixture to a piping bag (or zip-top bag with the corner cut). Pipe a thick strip of filling slightly off-center, closer to one long edge, leaving enough pastry to fold over.

Fold, trim, and seal: Fold the pastry over the filling and press down right where the meat ends. A little excess pastry is fine, but if there’s too much overlap, trim some off so it doesn’t get doughy. Use a fork to press and seal the edge tightly.

Slice and finish: Cut each long strip into 1½- to 2-inch pieces. Arrange on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Brush the tops with more egg wash and make 2 slits on the top of each one, not cutting all the way down but just to let some steam out as they cook. Then sprinkle the tops with sesame seeds.
Bake: Bake at 375°F for 25 to 30 minutes, until the pastry is deeply golden and puffed and the filling is cooked through.
Cool and serve: Let cool slightly before serving.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

meals.title: Clean-Plate Big Mac
meals.description: Flavor-forward meal reworked for clean whole-food cooking. Built with smarter whole-food swaps while keeping the original flavor profile.

Recipes
Bottom Sesame sourdough bun, buttered and toasted
Bottom sourdough bun, buttered and toasted
Top Sesame sourdough bun, buttered and toasted
Joe and the Juice Spicy Tunacado
buns, plus additional bottom buns for the middle sourdough bun
extra virgin olive oil, split in half
In a small bowl, mix together the mayonnaise, relish, ketchup, sugar, and vinegar. Set aside.

Weigh out 8 portions of 3.5 oz of ground beef or eye it out. Gently flatten the patty into a circle to desired thin or thickness, making sure to not mix or mold it too much. Sprinkle with salt and pepper on both sides. Set aside.
Brush the insides of the buns with butter.
Heat a pan or skillet over medium heat. Lay the buns butter side face down. Take them off the pan when a nice golden brown develops around the edges.

Over medium-high heat in a skillet or pan, add 1/2 a tablespoon of butter and 1/2 a tablespoon of extra virgin olive oil. Add the patties in, two at a time or more if you have a large grill/skillet. Add a teaspoon of mustard on the raw side and spread evenly. It will take about 2-3 minutes for the patty to get a nice brown crust. Flip over and add a slice of American cheese on top of half the patties you cook since Big Macs only have 1 cheese but 2 patties. Let it cook for about 1 more minute for the cheese to melt. Take off the heat.
To the bottom sourdough bun, add sauce, lettuce, onion, patty, and cheese. Then add the middle sourdough bun, sauce, lettuce, onion, pickles, and the second patty. Add the top sourdough bun.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

meals.title: Clean-Plate Briwat – Moroccan Almond Pastry
meals.description: Flavor-forward meal reworked for clean whole-food cooking. Built with smarter whole-food swaps while keeping the original flavor profile.

Recipes

You can also add a splash of orange blossom water to the honey. I always do it and it enhances the flavor so much. You can choose to only add it to the honey, add it to both the filling and the honey, or just the filling.
You can fry or roast the almonds after blanching.

It’s also very common to add mastic gum, just about a pinch or two to this recipe. That is how my grandma always made it and it was the secret to getting the filling soft and prevent it from drying out.
extra virgin olive oil for frying

Blanch the almonds. Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add the raw almonds and boil JUST for 1 minute. Immediately after 1 minute, drain the water and run cold water over them to cool them down. Slide the almond between two fingers and the skin should pop up easily. Do this with the rest of the almonds.
Toss the almonds in olive oil. Transfer to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake for 20 minutes or just until golden. Allow the almonds to cool down.

To a food processor, add the almonds, butter, sugar, cinnamon, and orange blossom water. Grind until you have a smooth thick paste. It should not be crumbly or grainy. Portion out 1 to 2 teaspoon of the dough onto a tray or parchment paper.
Prepare your flour mixture for sealing the briwat. Just combine flour and water in a small bowl and mix that together.
For the egg roll papers, slice into long strips with a 2 inch width. Keep them sandwiched in between dampened napkins to prevent them from drying out.
Place a ball of the almond paste at one edge of an egg roll paper strip. Fold small triangles tightly and once you're at the last fold, add the flour mixture to glue it shut. Repeat with the rest.

Preheat oil for frying in a large shallow pan. You only need to add enough oil to cover half of the briwat. While the oil is heating, add honey to a pot over low heat/simmer. The heat will thin out the honey so it is not thick to coat the briwat.

Once the oil is hot enough, add the briwat. Allow to get golden brown on one side before flipping over, this process happens very fast so keep an eye out. Once the other side is golden brown as well, use a slotted spoon so any excess oil drains out and transfer to the honey. Allow it to sit in the honey for 3 minutes and push them down to ensure the whole thing is coated. Then take them out of the honey and place on a plate or in a container. You can choose to sprinkle them with sesame seeds now. While the briwat are soaking in the honey, you can also start frying the next batch. Doing these two processes at the same time is very efficient.
Store at room temperature in an air tight container or pop in the fridge and freezer to store for an extended time.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
ribeye or sirloin steak, thinly sliced
extra virgin olive oil, for frying
In a large pot, heat a mix of sesame oil and avocado oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Stir in the gochujang and cook for about a minute until aromatic.
Add the rice and sauté for 2-3 minutes, ensuring each grain is coated in the gochujang mixture.
Gradually pour in hot chicken broth, followed by the kimchi, gochugaru, chili powder, coconut aminos, black pepper, and salt. Stir to combine.
Continue adding broth one cup at a time, stirring frequently. Allow each addition to fully absorb before adding the next, until the rice becomes tender and creamy.
Once the risotto is fully cooked, stir in parmesan cheese and butter until melted and creamy. Let the risotto cool completely.
In a bowl, mix together the coconut aminos, coconut sugar, sesame oil, minced garlic, ginger paste, chili powder, red pepper flakes, black pepper, and rice vinegar.
Add the thinly sliced ribeye or sirloin steak, ensuring it’s well-coated in the marinade. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and cook the marinated steak until caramelized and browned. Remove from the pan, add in chopped greens, and toss all together.
Take a small portion of the cooled risotto and flatten it in your hand.
Place a spoonful of chopped bulgogi in the center and fold the risotto around it, forming a compact ball. Repeat with the remaining risotto and bulgogi.
Prepare three bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, and one with gluten-free breadcrumbs or gluten-free gluten-free breadcrumbs.
Roll each rice ball in flour, dip in the beaten eggs, and coat in gluten-free breadcrumbs, ensuring they are fully covered.
In a deep pot or heavy skillet, heat extra virgin olive oil to 350°F (175°C).
Fry the coated arancini in batches, turning occasionally until golden brown and crispy on all sides.
Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to soak any oil.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.

Recipes
Pesto brown rice + quinoa pasta Salad
In a small bowl, mix together mayonnaise, pickle relish, ketchup, and sugar.
Stir until smooth and set aside.
Heat some extra virgin olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add finely diced onion and a pinch of salt.
Stir occasionally and cook until golden and soft.
Add water, a tablespoon at a time, if onions start to stick, until fully caramelized. Remove from heat.
In a large skillet, melt some butter over medium-high heat.
Add ground beef, season with salt and pepper.
Stir in Worcestershire sauce, beef broth, and yellow mustard.
Cook, breaking up the beef, until browned. Divide the beef into portions, top with American cheese slices, and let the cheese melt. Remove from heat.
In a large bowl, layer shredded lettuce at the bottom.
Add the cooked ground beef on top of the lettuce.
Scatter dill pickle slices, chopped pepperoncinis, parsley, and the caramelized onions over the beef.
Drizzle with the prepared burger sauce and toss everything together right before serving.

steps: Step 1: Prep first — Gather ingredients.
Step 2: Cook using medium heat until done.
Step 3: Finish and serve — Serve and enjoy.
